Package cc.quaternion.potshaper

Class PotColor

java.lang.Object
cc.quaternion.potshaper.PotColor
public class PotColor extends Object
Represents an immutable color with red, green, blue, and alpha components.

This class provides a comprehensive set of operations for color creation, conversion, and manipulation. It supports construction from RGB, RGBA, hexadecimal strings, and HSL values. Instances can be converted to AWT Color objects, hexadecimal representations, and integer packed formats. The class also includes utilities for adjusting opacity, lightening, darkening, mixing, and computing complementary or grayscale versions of a color.

All color component values are clamped to the valid range of 0-255 upon construction. The class defines a set of commonly used color constants for convenience.

Since:
1.0
Author:
Quaternion8192

  • Constructor Details

    • PotColor

      public PotColor(int red, int green, int blue)
      Constructs an opaque color from red, green, and blue components.

      The alpha component is set to 255 (fully opaque). Each component value is clamped to the range 0-255 inclusive.

      Parameters:
      red - the red component (0-255)
      green - the green component (0-255)
      blue - the blue component (0-255)

    • PotColor

      public PotColor(int red, int green, int blue, int alpha)
      Constructs a color from red, green, blue, and alpha components.

      Each component value is clamped to the range 0-255 inclusive.

      Parameters:
      red - the red component (0-255)
      green - the green component (0-255)
      blue - the blue component (0-255)
      alpha - the alpha (transparency) component (0-255)

  • Method Details

    • rgb

      public static PotColor rgb(int red, int green, int blue)
      Creates an opaque PotColor from red, green, and blue components.

      This is a convenience static factory method equivalent to the constructor PotColor(int, int, int).

      Usage Example

      
 PotColor brightRed = PotColor.rgb(255, 50, 50);
      Parameters:
      red - the red component (0-255)
      green - the green component (0-255)
      blue - the blue component (0-255)
      Returns:
      a new opaque PotColor instance
      See Also:
      • PotColor(int, int, int)

    • rgba

      public static PotColor rgba(int red, int green, int blue, int alpha)
      Creates a PotColor from red, green, blue, and alpha components.

      This is a convenience static factory method equivalent to the constructor PotColor(int, int, int, int).

      Usage Example

      
 PotColor semiTransparentBlue = PotColor.rgba(0, 0, 255, 128);
      Parameters:
      red - the red component (0-255)
      green - the green component (0-255)
      blue - the blue component (0-255)
      alpha - the alpha component (0-255)
      Returns:
      a new PotColor instance with the specified transparency
      See Also:
      • PotColor(int, int, int, int)

    • hex

      public static PotColor hex(String hex)
      Creates a PotColor from a hexadecimal color string.

      The input string can be in the following formats:

      • 6-digit RGB: "FF0000" or "#FF0000"
      • 8-digit RGBA: "FF000080" or "#FF000080"
      • 3-digit shorthand RGB: "F00" or "#F00" (expanded to "FF0000")
      If the string is null, empty, or cannot be parsed, the method returns BLACK. The parsing is case-insensitive.

      Usage Example

      
 PotColor color1 = PotColor.hex("#FF5733");
 PotColor color2 = PotColor.hex("00FF00");
 PotColor color3 = PotColor.hex("#C8F"); // Expands to "#CC88FF"
      Parameters:
      hex - the hexadecimal color string, optionally prefixed with '#'
      Returns:
      a PotColor corresponding to the hex string, or BLACK on parse failure

    • hsl

      public static PotColor hsl(double hue, double saturation, double lightness)
      Creates a PotColor from HSL (Hue, Saturation, Lightness) values.

      The resulting color is fully opaque (alpha = 255). The conversion follows the standard HSL to RGB algorithm.

      Usage Example

      
 // A pure red hue, fully saturated, medium lightness
 PotColor redHsl = PotColor.hsl(0, 100, 50);
      Parameters:
      hue - the hue angle in degrees (0-360)
      saturation - the saturation percentage (0-100)
      lightness - the lightness percentage (0-100)
      Returns:
      a new opaque PotColor derived from the HSL values

    • fromAwtColor

      public static PotColor fromAwtColor(Color color)
      Creates a PotColor from an AWT Color object.

      If the provided color is null, the method returns BLACK.

      Usage Example

      
 Color awtColor = new Color(255, 0, 0, 128);
 PotColor potColor = PotColor.fromAwtColor(awtColor);
      Parameters:
      color - the source AWT Color, may be null
      Returns:
      a PotColor with the same components as the AWT Color, or BLACK if input is null
      See Also:
      • toAwtColor()

    • getRed

      public int getRed()
      Returns the red component of this color.
      Returns:
      the red component value (0-255)

    • getGreen

      public int getGreen()
      Returns the green component of this color.
      Returns:
      the green component, in the range 0-255.

    • getBlue

      public int getBlue()
      Returns the blue component of this color.
      Returns:
      the blue component, in the range 0-255.

    • getAlpha

      public int getAlpha()
      Returns the alpha (transparency) component of this color.
      Returns:
      the alpha component, in the range 0-255.

    • getOpacity

      public double getOpacity()
      Returns the opacity of this color as a floating-point value.

      The opacity is derived from the alpha component, where 0.0 represents full transparency and 1.0 represents full opacity.

      Returns:
      the opacity value, between 0.0 and 1.0 inclusive.

    • toAwtColor

      public Color toAwtColor()
      Converts this color to a standard AWT Color object.
      Returns:
      a new Color instance with the same RGBA components.

    • toHex

      public String toHex()
      Returns a hexadecimal string representation of the RGB components.

      The format is six uppercase hexadecimal digits (RRGGBB), without a leading hash.

      Returns:
      the RGB hex string, e.g., "FF0000" for red.

    • toHexWithHash

      public String toHexWithHash()
      Returns a hexadecimal string representation of the RGB components with a leading hash.

      The format is a hash followed by six uppercase hexadecimal digits (#RRGGBB).

      Returns:
      the RGB hex string with a hash prefix, e.g., "#FF0000".

    • toHexRgba

      public String toHexRgba()
      Returns a hexadecimal string representation of the RGBA components.

      The format is eight uppercase hexadecimal digits (RRGGBBAA).

      Returns:
      the RGBA hex string, e.g., "FF0000FF" for opaque red.

    • toRgbInt

      public int toRgbInt()
      Returns an integer representation of the RGB components.

      The integer is packed as 0xRRGGBB, where the most significant byte is red.

      Returns:
      the RGB integer value.

    • toArgbInt

      public int toArgbInt()
      Returns an integer representation of the ARGB components.

      The integer is packed as 0xAARRGGBB, where the most significant byte is alpha.

      Returns:
      the ARGB integer value.

    • withAlpha

      public PotColor withAlpha(int alpha)
      Creates a new color with a different alpha component.
      Parameters:
      alpha - the new alpha value, in the range 0-255.
      Returns:
      a new PotColor instance with the specified alpha.

    • withOpacity

      public PotColor withOpacity(double opacity)
      Creates a new color with a different opacity.
      Parameters:
      opacity - the new opacity value, in the range 0.0 (transparent) to 1.0 (opaque).
      Returns:
      a new PotColor instance with the specified opacity.

    • lighter

      public PotColor lighter(double factor)
      Creates a lighter version of this color.

      Each RGB component is moved linearly towards 255 (white) by the specified factor. The alpha component remains unchanged.

      Parameters:
      factor - the lightening factor, between 0.0 (no change) and 1.0 (pure white).
      Returns:
      a new, lighter PotColor instance.

    • darker

      public PotColor darker(double factor)
      Creates a darker version of this color.

      Each RGB component is scaled down (multiplied by (1 - factor)). The alpha component remains unchanged.

      Parameters:
      factor - the darkening factor, between 0.0 (no change) and 1.0 (pure black).
      Returns:
      a new, darker PotColor instance.

    • mix

      public PotColor mix(PotColor other, double factor)
      Linearly interpolates between this color and another color.

      The resulting color is calculated as: this + (other - this) * factor. All components (RGBA) are interpolated independently.

      Parameters:
      other - the target color to mix with.
      factor - the interpolation factor, where 0.0 yields this color and 1.0 yields the other color.
      Returns:
      a new PotColor representing the mixture.

    • complement

      public PotColor complement()
      Returns the complementary color.

      The complement is calculated by subtracting each RGB component from 255. The alpha component remains unchanged.

      Returns:
      a new PotColor that is the complement of this color.

    • toGrayscale

      public PotColor toGrayscale()
      Converts this color to grayscale using a standard luminance formula.

      The grayscale value is calculated as: 0.299*R + 0.587*G + 0.114*B. The alpha component remains unchanged.

      Returns:
      a new grayscale PotColor instance.

    • equals

      public boolean equals(Object o)
      Overrides:
      equals in class Object

    • hashCode

      public int hashCode()
      Overrides:
      hashCode in class Object

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
